nodes.title: Information Technology
nodes.description: An overview of the functional areas of business and the importance of information technology to the success of the organization with coverage of essential communication, problem solving and productivity tools employed in the modern enterprise. Prerequisites: Business Administration major.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major. New first-Year BSAD majors only.

nodes.title: Business Admin Honors Thesis
nodes.description: Honors thesis dealing with business administration topics. Honors College students only. Prerequisites: BUS 2996; Business Administration Honors College student.
nodes.other_requirements: Honors College students only. Business Administration Honors College student. Asynchronous online; BSAD HCOL Students only; Instructor Permission Required.

nodes.title: Individual Taxation
nodes.description: Highlights federal income tax concepts and rules applicable to individuals. Examines how the federal tax system accounts for items of income and expense in computing taxable income, considering both personal and business transactions. Prerequisites: BUS 1610, BUS 2620; Business Administration major, minor, co-major or Accounting minor. Pre/co-requisites: BUS 1610, BUS 2620.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, minor, co-major or Accounting minor. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, and BSAD or ACCT minor only.

nodes.title: Business Law II
nodes.description: Concepts of law as related to business, including law of contracts, sales, bailment, and negotiable instruments, business and law agency, partnerships, and corporations. BUS 2370 is not a prerequisite for BUS 2380. Prerequisites: BUS 1110, BUS 1101, ECON 1400, or ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business major or minor; Law & Society minors by Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum grade of C- in prerequisites; Business major or minor; Law & Society minors by Instructor permission. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major only. Open to degree and PACE students; Instructor permission, email gsbcss@uvm.edu.

nodes.title: Business Communications
nodes.description: Provides a basic understanding of professional business communications. Prerequisite: Business Administration major; First-year/Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major; First-year/Sophomore standing. BSAD majors only.

nodes.title: Personal Finance & Investing
nodes.description: Analyze the process for making personal financial decisions; develop personal financial goals in view of an individual's background and emotions related to money, debt, spending habits, risk taking etc.; assess economic factors that influence financial planning.

nodes.title: Real Estate Finance
nodes.description: This course is an introduction of real estate finance and investments. Topics include urban economics, appraisal, investment value analysis, financing, and development. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Basic Statistical Methods 1
nodes.description: Foundational course for students taking further quantitative courses. Exploratory data analysis, probability distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing. Introductory regression, experimentation, contingency tables, and nonparametrics. Computer software used. Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for more than one of STAT 1410 or STAT 2430. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Human Resource Management
nodes.description: Critical examination of contemporary problems in human resource management; including job analysis, recruitment, training and employee development, health and safety, compensation, performance appraisal, and related topics. Prerequisites: BUS 2300; Business Administration major or minor; minimum Junior standing; Master of Accountancy Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor; minimum Junior standing; Master of Accountancy Graduate student. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only.

nodes.title: Free Markets & Free Enterprise
nodes.description: Study of level and structure of interest rates and characteristics of financial institutions and markets. Topics include market vs. natural rate of interest, interest rate structure, behavior of interest rates. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C in BUS 2800; Business Administration major or minor; BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: Tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Business Administration major; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major; BSAD majors only. Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Supply Chain Management
nodes.description: Explores how firms can organize supply chains to more effectively align supply with the demand for products. Prerequisites: BUS 2700; Business Administration major or minor; Engineering Management major, or Master of Accountancy Graduate Student; other majors or minors by Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor; Engineering Management major, or Master of Accountancy Graduate Student; other majors or minors by Instructor permission. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major, EMGT major, and ACTG graduate students. Graduates must have permission from Grad College. Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Security Val & Portfolio Mgmt
nodes.description: Examination of theories and evidence on the investment decision process including operations of equity securities markets, market efficiency, financial asset prices, and portfolio management. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; STAT 1410, STAT 2430, or PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2010 with a minimum grade of a C-; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C in BUS 2800; minimum grade of C- in STAT 1410, STAT 2430, or PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2010; Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major and minor, and 2BUS Co-Majors. Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Prof. Development Series I
nodes.description: Seminar series focusing on engagement, career preparedness, and information literacy. Prerequisite: First-Year Business Administration major.
nodes.other_requirements: First-Year Business Administration major. New first-Year BSAD majors only. Open to returning students on August 14. Though most of the instruction will be synchronous online, students expected to attend in-person Career Readiness and Grossman School of Business Events throughout semester.

nodes.title: Sustainable Bus Strategies
nodes.description: Focus on the basics of strategic management to understand business choices and the sustainability of business organizations and their stakeholders. Prerequisites: ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration major or minor. Co-requisite: BUS 1610.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C-; Business Administration major or minor. Co-requisite: BUS 1610 . BSAD majors or BSAD minors only; Course Fee $60

nodes.title: Bus. Driven Decision Making
nodes.description: Using Microsoft Excel and Tableau software, students will solve realistic business scenarios in areas related to finance, accounting, production and operations, sales and marketing, producing interactive data visualizations focused on business intelligence. Prerequisite: BUS 1140 with a minimum grade of C-, BUS 2740, or CS 1210; Business Administration or Computer Science and Information Systems major or Business Administration minor.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C- in BUS 1140 or CS 1210; Business Administration or Computer Science and Information Systems major or Business Administration minor. BSAD, CSIS majors, 2BUS co-majors, or BSAD minors only; Open to degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Sustainable Bus Strategic Cap
nodes.description: Integrative, capstone course concerned with issues and decisions facing senior management in the thematic area of Sustainable Business. Prerequisites: BUS 2150 with a minimum grade of C-, BUS 2300, BUS 2500, BUS 2700, BUS 2800; Business Administration majors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration majors only. BSAD majors only. BUS 2150 with a minimum grade of C-

nodes.title: Leadership & Org Behavior
nodes.description: How people in organizations think and behave. Focuses on how leadership and motivation affect individuals and teams in the workplace and a global business context. Prerequisites: BUS 1110 or BUS 1120 or ECON 1400 or ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C- or Instructor permission; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Engineering Management, Dietetics, Nutrition & Food Sciences major; Business Administration minor, Sports Management minor or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: with a minimum grade of C- or Instructor permission; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Engineering Management, Dietetics, Nutrition & Food Sciences major; Business Administration minor, Sports Management minor or Instructor permission. BSAD, CSIS, DNFS, EMGT majors, 2BUS Co-major, and BSAD, SMGT minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students; Prereqs enforced by the system: with a minimum grade of C-.

nodes.title: Leading Sustainable Enterprise
nodes.description: Topics and material that may develop later into a regular course offering; in addition, it may include topics and material offered only once. Prerequisite: Business Administration Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration Graduate student. MAcc graduate students only

nodes.title: Professional Experience
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Concurrent internship, Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Concurrent internship, Instructor permission. BSAD majors or BSAD minors only; Application and concurrent internship required; Instructor permission required; For permission email gsbcss@uvm.edu;

nodes.title: Corporate Retail Seminar
nodes.description: Offers an in-depth study of the strategies, practices and challenges at play for the world's largest corporate retailers. Seminar style based on active discussion and participation. Focuses on decision making and planning, covers topics including the evolution of corporate retailing, location and site selection, supply chain strategies, customer relationship management and data driven decision making, merchandise management, branding and communications strategies and visual merchandising and design. Prerequisites: BUS 3560; Business Administration major, minor, co-major, or Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisites: BUS 3560.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, minor, co-major, or Instructor permission. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major.

nodes.title: Business Savvy
nodes.description: Introduces non-business majors to the fundamentals of accounting, finance, marketing, operations, management, strategy. May be used by Business Administration minors to fulfill three credits of upper-level Business Administration electives. Prerequisites: Non-Business Administration major.
nodes.other_requirements: Non-Business Administration major. Reserved for Innov & Ent Learning Comm Course. Not open to business majors. Open to all non-business majors and PACE students on August 14.

nodes.title: Consumer Behavior
nodes.description: Exploration and analysis of research evidence from marketing and behavioral science relevant to a theory of consumer behavior. Emphasis also given to research methodologies. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major.

nodes.title: Decision Analysis
nodes.description: Introduces the tools and techniques necessary for effective decision-making in business organizations operating in a complex and dynamic environment. Prerequisites: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234 with a minimum grade of C-; STAT 1410 or STAT 2430 with a minimum grade of C-, or PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2010 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Computer Science and Information Systems, Engineering Management major, or Business Administration minor.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C- in MATH 1212 or MATH 1234, minimum grade of C- in STAT 1410 or STAT 2430, minimum grade of C- in PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2010, Business Administration, Computer Science and Information Systems, Engineering Management major, or Business Administration minor. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major, CSIS, and EMGT major.

nodes.title: Prof. Development Series III
nodes.description: Seminar series focusing on engagement and professional development with a focus on transitioning from University life to the work world. Prerequisites: BUS 2102; Business Administration major; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major; minimum Junior standing. BSAD majors only; Though most of the instruction will be synchronous online, students are expected to attend in-person Career Readiness and Grossman School of Business Events throughout semester.

nodes.title: Entrepreneurial Family Firms
nodes.description: Students will learn to work effectively in and with family enterprises - the predominant organizational form in the world. By understanding their unique advantages and challenges, students will learn to develop strategic solutions to improve the family and business performance. Prerequisites: BUS 2300; Business Administration, Engineering Management major, or Business Administration minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration, Engineering Management major, or Business Administration minor. BSAD, EGMT major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Tax & Entrepreneurial Ventures
nodes.description: Explores tax and other aspects of using partnerships, S corporations, and limited liability companies for large and small business ventures. Considers formation, funding, and exit strategies for startups; structuring options for private equity, venture capital, and other investors; and pass-through treatments for these very common and increasingly popular business entities.

nodes.title: Integrated Product Dev
nodes.description: An applied, project-based course focused on how products are conceived, designed, and manufactured using real-world product development practices. Prerequisite: Senior standing. Cross-listed with: ME 3060, BUS 3360.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing

nodes.title: Principles of Macroeconomics
nodes.description: Introduction to economic concepts, institutions, and analysis, particularly as related to the economy as a whole. May be taught with traditional approach or with strong mathematical emphasis.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students. Knowledge of high school algebra.

nodes.title: Psych Research Methods
nodes.description: Principles of research methodology, including design and reporting. Prepares students to understand and evaluate psychological research in a variety of areas of psychology. Credit not awarded for both PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2000. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: NEUR majors and minors only

nodes.title: International Finance Mgmt
nodes.description: Theories and practices of international financial management examined. Topics investigated include: systems of international exchange, spot and forward markets, and expropriation and exchange risk. Prerequisites: ECON 1400 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C- in ECON 1400; Business Administration major or minor.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to limits and differential/integral calculus with a wide variety of applications. Students interested in intensive use of mathematics should take MATH 1234. Credit not awarded for both MATH 1212 and MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248. See MATH 1242. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system: MATH 1012 or MATH 1034 or ALEKS MPA of at least 61; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Accounting Information Systems
nodes.description: Examination of how accounting information is collected, stored and made available to decision makers with an emphasis on internal control implementation. Credit not awarded for both BUS 3660 and BUS 5660. Prerequisites: BUS 3610; Business Administration major, Business Administration or Accounting minor, Master of Accountancy Graduate Student.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, Business Administration or Accounting minor, Master of Accountancy Graduate Student. BSAD Major, 2BUS Co-Major, BSAD or ACCT minors, ACTG graduate students.

nodes.title: Digital Marketing
nodes.description: Teaches the ways in which digital tools and multiple platforms have created a wide range of marketing options for organizations. Theoretical strategy, professional engagements, and hands-on practice will illustrate the strategic reasons for utilizing digital marketing and how to use the tools most effectively. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Auditing
nodes.description: Examination of auditing theory and practice. Topics include standards, ethics and legal responsibilities of the profession, audit planning, internal control, audit evidence, and auditor communications. Prerequisites: BUS 3611, BUS 3660; Business Administration major, Master of Accountancy student, Business Administration minor, or Accounting minor; minimum Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, Master of Accountancy student, Business Administration minor, or Accounting minor; minimum Senior standing. MAcc Graduate students only; Undergraduate BSAD students by permission; For permission email gsbcss@uvm.edu.

nodes.title: Principles of Microeconomics
nodes.description: Study of individual economic units with particular emphasis on market interactions among firms and households.

nodes.title: Prof. Development Series II
nodes.description: Seminar series focusing on engagement, career preparedness and professional development. Prerequisites: BUS 1102; Business Adminstration major; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major; minimum Sophomore standing. BSAD majors and 2BUS Co-Major.

nodes.title: Governmental Accounting
nodes.description: Provides a study of the theory and practical application of accounting principles and auditing standards to governmental entities. Prerequisite: Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission. BSAD majors or MAcc graduate students only. Minimum senior or graduate standing.

nodes.title: Wall Street Seminar
nodes.description: Application of financial theory to stock/bond valuation, credit analysis, security underwriting, or risk management. Students will complete projects assigned by major financial service firms. May be repeated; only counts once toward Business Administration major or minor. Prerequisites: BUS 2810; Business Administration major or minor; Instructor permission required.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor; Instructor permission required. Instructor permission required; May be repeated; Only counts once toward BSAD major or minor; BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only.

nodes.title: Accounting Rsch, Reg & Ethics
nodes.description: Addresses current accounting issues and the roles of various regulators that impact the accounting profession. Class material emphasizes the ethical responsibilities of professional accountants. Prerequisite: Master of Accountancy student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Accountancy student. MAcc Graduate Students Only: Students will explore financial reporting issues, regulatory requirements, and ethical responsibilities of professional accountants. This Master of Accountancy capstone course is a conducted as a seminar where students are expected to participate in class discussion and activities. It fulfills the Graduate College comprehensive exam requirement.

nodes.title: Corporate Financial Reporting1
nodes.description: Study of how corporations account for and present the results of their financial activities. Emphasizes accounting for assets, current liabilities, and the related revenue and expenses. Provides overview of the four primary financial statements and accompanying notes. Prerequisites: BUS 1610, BUS 2620, both with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration major; or Business Administration or Accounting minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Both BUS 1610 and BUS 2620 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration major; or Business Administration or Accounting minor; CSIS majors by Instructor permission; For permission email: gsbcss@uvm.edu; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Sustainable Family Enter I
nodes.description: Long-lived family firms that sustain over generations of leaders, economic and industry life cycles, embrace transgenerational entrepreneurship and innovation as part of their culture. This course focuses on the unique dynamics and dilemmas of these family businesses.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Cooperative Education
nodes.description: Designed to support students in gaining maximum value from their co-operative education positions in relation to their academic experiences. There will be assignments during the co-op term, administered through Brightspace, to help students reflect on professional experience they are gaining.

nodes.title: Financial Accounting
nodes.description: Introduction to the accounting system and generally accepted accounting principles that govern income determination and financial position presentation. Credit not awarded for both BUS 1610 and BUS 1600. Prerequisites: ECON 1400 or ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Dietetics, Nutrition & Food Science, Engineering Management major, Business Administration, Accounting minor.
nodes.other_requirements: with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Dietetics, Nutrition & Food Science, Engineering Management major, Business Administration, Accounting minor. BSAD major, 2BUS Co-Major, ACCT & BSAD minor, CSIS, and EMGT majors.

nodes.title: Green Mountain Investment Fund
nodes.description: Involves practical and real time operation of an investment fund. Covers the steps necessary to fill a role as an analyst or portfolio manager of a traditional long-only money management operation. May repeated with Instructor permission; only counts once toward Business Administration major or minor. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor. May be repeated with Instructor permission; Only counts once toward BSAD major or minor; Prereqs enforced by the system:BUS 2800with a minimum grade of C; BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Real Estate Finance in Pract
nodes.description: Advanced courses on topics beyond the scope of existing departmental offerings. See Schedule of Courses for specific titles and prerequisites. Prerequisite: Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. Others by permission, email gsbcss@uvm.edu.

nodes.title: Business Process Improvement
nodes.description: Familiarizes students with the basic conceptual issues of continuously improving business processes to compete more effectively on quality, time, and cost. Prerequisites: BUS 1140, BUS 2740, or CS 1210; Business Administration, Engineering Management, Computer Science & Information Systems major, Business Administration minor by Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration, Engineering Management, Computer Science & Information Systems major, Business Administration minor by Instructor permission. BSAD, CSIS, EMGT major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minor only; Open to degree and PACE students; Prereq enforced by the system:BUS 1140orCS 1210.

nodes.title: Ind Study - Business Planning
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: MAcc Graduate students only; Instructor permission required; Variable credit 1-3

nodes.title: Global Business Strategic Cap
nodes.description: Integrative, capstone course concerned with issues and decisions facing senior management in the thematic area of Global Business. Prerequisites: BUS 2150 with a minimum grade of C-, BUS 2300, BUS 2500, BUS 2700, BUS 2800; Business Administration majors only; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: BUS 2150 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration majors only; Senior standing.

nodes.title: Managerial Accounting
nodes.description: Introduction to use of accounting for planning, cost behavior, budgeting, analysis, and decision making. Prerequisites: BUS 1610 with a minimum grade of C-; ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Engineering Management, Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Sciences, Computer Science & Information Systems major, Business Administration, Accounting minor by Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: BUS 1610 with a minimum grade of C-; ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Engineering Management, Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Sciences, Computer Science & Information Systems major, Business Administration, Accounting minor by Instructor permission. BSAD, CSIS, DNFS, EMGT majors, 2BUS co-majors, and BSAD, ACCT minors only; Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Business Law I
nodes.description: Concepts of law as related to business, including law of contracts, sales, bailments, and negotiable instruments, business and laws of agency, partnerships, and corporations. This course is not a prerequisite for BUS 2380. Prerequisites: BUS 1110, BUS 1101, ECON 1400, or ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; Business major or minor; Law & Society minors by Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: with a minimum grade of C-; Business major or minor; Law & Society minors by Instructor permission. BSAD majors, BSAD minors and 2BUS co-major only. Open to degree and PACE students; Open to L&S minors on August 14.

nodes.title: International Management
nodes.description: Exploration of international business environments and management issues corporations encounter in these environments. Topics include cross-cultural differences, international corporate strategy and structure, cross-cultural communication, negotiation, and human resource management. Prerequisites: BUS 2300; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major and minor, 2BUS Co-Major. Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: The Business Enterprise I
nodes.description: This fundamental course provides instruction in how businesses work and what is required to excel and lead in today's work environment. Prerequisite: First-Year Business Administration major.
nodes.other_requirements: First-Year Business Administration major. New first-Year BSAD majors only.

nodes.title: Intermediate Financial Mgmt
nodes.description: Examines key areas of financial decision making. With cases and problems, issues such as capital budgeting, leasing, mergers, and acquisitions examined. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, and BSAD minors only; Open to degree and PACE students; Prereq enforced by the system

nodes.title: Operations Management
nodes.description: Introduces decisions related to the design, management, and improvement of activities that create and deliver a firm's products and services. Prerequisites: BUS 2130 with a minimum grade of C-, BUS 1610 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Engineering Management, Computer Science & Information Systems major or Business Administration minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration, Engineering Management, Computer Science & Information Systems major or Business Administration minor. BSAD, CSIS, EMGT majors, 2BUS co-major, and BSAD minors only; Open to degree and PACE students. Minimum grade of C- required for BUS 2130 and BUS 1610.

nodes.title: Fraud Examination
nodes.description: Covers all of the major methods employees use to commit occupational fraud. Students will learn how and why occupational fraud is committed, how fraudulent conduct can be deterred, and how allegations of fraud should be investigated. Prerequisite: BUS 3611.
nodes.other_requirements: MAcc students only; Undergrads by permission; For permission email gsbcss@uvm.edu.

nodes.title: Acct Research & Communications
nodes.description: Addresses components of professional communications and research skills key to accounting career success. Clear business writing, effective presentations, and group meeting communications are emphasized and illustrated through a variety of assignments. Prerequisite: Master of Accountancy student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Accountancy student or Instructor permission. Part of Term A; MAcc Graduate students only

nodes.title: Marketing Research
nodes.description: The role of research in a marketing information framework. Emphasis on survey research, data collection, and analysis. Experimental designs also examined. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. Prereqs enforced by the system: BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minor only; Open to MACC students if approved by the Graduate College; Contact the Graduate College for information.

nodes.title: Marketing Management
nodes.description: The place of marketing in our economy. Analysis of the market structure by function, institutions, and commodities. Consumer and organizational activities reviewed. Prerequisites: ECON 1450 with a minimum grade of C-; STAT 1410 or STAT 2430 with a minimum grade of C- or PSYS 2002 with a minimum grade of C- and PSYS 2010 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Engineering Management majors; Business Administration minor; Sports Management minor with Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Engineering Management majors; Business Administration minor; Sports Management minor with Instructor permission. BSAD, CSIS, EMGT majors, 2BUS co-majors, or BSAD minors only. Open to degree and PACE students; For instructor permission, email gsbcss@uvm.edu. Cannot earn credit for both CDAE 2680 and BUS 2500

nodes.title: Marketing Communications
nodes.description: Emphasizes the coordination of advertising and sales promotion into cohesive promotional programs. Stresses the need to integrate promotional activity into the overall marketing strategy. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Comp Prog I: for CS majors
nodes.description: Introduction to algorithmic problem solving and computer programming. Designed to provide a foundation for further studies in computer science.

nodes.title: Green Oper. and Supply Chains
nodes.description: Study of the foundational concepts in supply chain and operations management in sustainable enterprises. Design, planning, and control are examined, with emphasis on managerial analysis and decision making that will help the enterprise succeed responsibly and sustainably.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate students only; Asynchronous online course; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Statistics for Engineering
nodes.description: Data analysis, probability models, parameter estimation, hypothesis testing. Multi- factor experimental design and regression analysis. Quality control, SPC, reliability. Engineering cases and project. Statistical analysis software. Credit not awarded for both STAT 1410 and STAT 2430. Prerequisites: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum sophomore standing; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: The Business Enterprise II
nodes.description: Provides an understanding of what managers do on a daily basis and the issues they face in running a business; including a basic introduction to the functional areas of business and the types of decisions involved. Prerequisite: BUS 1110 with a minimum grade of C-.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum grade of C-; BSAD majors only.

nodes.title: Retail Management
nodes.description: Provides an overview of retail management. Key perspectives that shape the field including strategic planning, merchandising, and competitive advantage are emphasized. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor, or Master of Accountancy Graduate Student.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor, or Master of Accountancy Graduate Student. Prereqs enforced by the system: BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to MACC students if approved by the Graduate College; Contact the Graduate College for information. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Financial Tech and Analytics
nodes.description: Provides a broad understanding of the financial technology landscape, including topics like: payment systems, lending platforms, robo-advising, cryptocurrency and blockchain, insurance and real estate technology, crowdfunding and artificial intelligence, and customer relationship management. Prerequisites: BUS 2130 with a minimum grade of C-; BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. BSAD Major or Minor, and 2BUS Co-major only.

nodes.title: Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to calculus of functions of one variable including: limits, continuity, techniques and applications of differentiation and integration. Credit not given for more than one course in the pair MATH 1212, MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 1248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system.

nodes.title: Intn'l Market Analysis
nodes.description: Examines the cultural, economic, historic, and political factors that affect the analysis of foreign markets. Specific attention is given to the processes by which market entry decisions are developed and implemented. Prerequisites: BUS 2500; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor.

nodes.title: Sustainable Family Enter II
nodes.description: Goes beyond the documented best practices of family enterprises embedding sustainable development goals into their core operations to innovative next practices being tried while still continuing to generate positive economic returns to sustain their enterprise. Case examples, experiential exercises, virtual learning, lectures, and discussions are used to bring concepts to life. Prerequisite: BUS 6450.
nodes.other_requirements: Grad students only; Part of Term A; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Ethics & Social Resp in Mgt
nodes.description: Engages students in reflections on the role and purpose of business organizations in society and questions the sense of human action in these business organizations in order to face future global challenges in a socially responsible and sustainable way. Prerequisites: BUS 1110 or BUS 2300; Business Administration major; Business Administration minor with Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major; Business Administration minor with Instructor permission. BSAD major or minor, or 2BUS co-major only.

nodes.title: Accounting & Reporting for ESG
nodes.description: Combines an introduction to financial and managerial accounting and reporting with the most recent standards used by entities worldwide to report on their environmental, social and governance (ESG) activities.

nodes.title: Protecting Ideas: Law & Innov
nodes.description: Specialized or experimental courses offered as resources permit. Prerequisite: Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minor only.

nodes.title: Financial Statement Analysis
nodes.description: Study of the concepts and techniques underlying corporate financial statement analysis, with an emphasis on equity valuation models. Prerequisites: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor; BSAD majors, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD, ACCT minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Corporate Financial Reporting2
nodes.description: Continuation of Corporate Financial Reporting1, with emphasis on accounting and reporting of liabilities, owners' equity and related effect on income determination of an enterprise. Prerequisites: BUS 3610; Business Administration major, Business Administration or Accounting minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, Business Administration or Accounting minor. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD, ACCT minors only; CSIS majors by Instructor permission; For permission email: gsbcss@uvm.edu; Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Options and Futures
nodes.description: Financial derivatives - options, futures, and swaps. Topics include: structures of the markets for exchange traded and over - THE counter derivatives, identification and exploitation of arbitrage opportunities, use and misuse of derivatives to hedge risk in both financial and product markets. Prerequisite: BUS 2800 with a minimum grade of C; Business Administration major or minor.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major or minor; BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, or BSAD minors only; Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Integrated Product Dev
nodes.description: An applied, project-based course focused on how products are conceived, designed, and manufactured using real-world product development practices. Prerequisite: Senior standing. Cross-listed with: ME 3060, EMGT 3061.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing. BSAD major or minor, 2BUS co-majors only.

nodes.title: Sustainable Marketing
nodes.description: Accelerated course on sustainable marketing principles and theory which focuses on how enterprises respond to the twin global challenges of global poverty and environmental sustainability.

nodes.title: Adv Topics in Management Acctg
nodes.description: Emphasizes use of internal and external information in management decision making; includes cost of inventory, business activities, strategic use of information, long-range planning. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both BUS 5620 and BUS 3620. Prerequisites: Master of Accountancy student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Accountancy student. BSAD major, 2BUS co-major, BSAD or ACCT minor, or MAcc graduate students only.

nodes.title: Managerial Finance
nodes.description: The financial function in the corporation. Techniques for evaluating current use of resources and proposed resource acquisitions or dispositions. Prerequisites: BUS 1610 with a minimum grade of C-; Business Administration, Computer Science & Information Systems, Engineering Management majors, or Business Administration minor.

nodes.title: Integrated Product Dev
nodes.description: An applied, project-based course focused on how products are conceived, designed, and manufactured using real-world product development practices. Prerequisite: Senior standing. Cross-listed with: BUS 3360, EMGT 3061.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing.

nodes.title: Advanced Accounting
nodes.description: Focuses on accounting for business combinations and developing consolidated financial statements. Includes accounting for foreign currency transactions, and foreign subsidiaries. Prerequisite: BUS 3611.
nodes.other_requirements: MAcc students only; Undergraduates by permission; for permission, email gsbcss@uvm.edu.

nodes.title: Corporation Taxation
nodes.description: A survey of the tax consequences for C corporations and their shareholders of womb-to-tomb transactions, which might include formations, acquisitions, divisions, consolidations, and international operations as well as the reporting of book/tax differences. Prerequisites: BUS 3610; Business Administration major, Master of Accountancy student, Business Administration minor, or Accounting minor; minimum Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Business Administration major, Master of Accountancy student, Business Administration minor, or Accounting minor; minimum Senior standing. BSAD majors, BSAD and ACCT minors and MAcc students; Minimum senior standing or graduate standing;

nodes.title: Entrepreneurship Capstone
nodes.description: Integrative, capstone course concerned with issues and decisions facing senior management in the thematic area of Entrepreneurship. Prerequisites: BUS 2150 with a minimum grade of C-, BUS 2300, BUS 2500, BUS 2700, BUS 2800; Business Administration majors only; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum grade of C- in BUS 2150; Business Administration majors only; Senior standing.
